subject: barn owls
one_liner: Workforce identity and access governance for nocturnal raptor populations.
brand_voice_notes: Enterprise identity vendor with a niche specialization in working barn-owl populations. Speaks about Tyto alba the way Okta speaks about workforce identity: provisioning, governance, attestation, audit. Sells compliance with a straight face. Pricing is confident and overspecified. The bit is that owl barns are evaluated for SOC 2 readiness and we have a Customer Success team for them.

hero.h1: Workforce identity, for the working barn.
hero.sub: Strix is the identity and access governance layer for nocturnal raptor populations. Provision, attest, and audit every Tyto alba on your property with the same rigor your security team applies to a quarterly close. SOC 2 Type II. Deployed at 14 working barns, two raptor sanctuaries, and one decommissioned belfry.

features.title: Federated Hoot Authentication
features.blurb: A seven-microphone perch array listens continuously to your barn and matches every vocalization against the registered hoot signature for that owl. Three consecutive non-matches silently revoke roost access and page the on-call ornithologist. Recovery requires a daylight visit from a Strix Customer Success representative.

features.title: Roost-Level Access Governance
features.blurb: Define policies down to the rafter. Juveniles are blocked from the upper loft until their fledgling milestone is attested by a licensed handler. Visiting owls receive sundown-to-sunrise credentials that auto-expire at first light. Every gate transition writes an immutable event to the audit ledger.

features.title: Automated Avian Lifecycle
features.blurb: From first molt to dispersal, Strix manages the full owl employment cycle. New fledglings are auto-provisioned the moment plumage maturity is detected by the camera array. Departing owls (dispersal, predation, retirement) are deprovisioned within four hours of confirmation, with a configurable seven-year retention window on their access logs.

pricing_tiers.name: Single Roost
pricing_tiers.tagline: One barn. Up to six owls. Monthly access review.
perks: 1 working barn, up to 6 Tyto alba
Hoot enrollment for the colony lead
Standard governance: 11 controls mapped to SOC 2

pricing_tiers.name: Federated Eaves
pricing_tiers.tagline: Up to twenty-four owls across four sites. Cross-barn SSO.
perks: Up to 24 Tyto alba across 4 sites
Quarterly Governance Attestation, countersigned by a vet

pricing_tiers.name: Sovereign Aviary
pricing_tiers.tagline: Unlimited birds. On-prem. Full residency control.
perks: Unlimited owls, unlimited species (raptors and corvids)
On-prem perch agents, fully air-gapped option
Reporting cadence set jointly with your veterinary lead
SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, USDA-aligned, FedRAMP Moderate in flight

testimonials.quote: We had three barn owls all named Ash. Strix was the first product that could tell them apart.
testimonials.name: Wren Halloran-Pike
testimonials.title: Operations Lead, Pike Family Working Barn

testimonials.quote: Our last audit took eleven hours and a forensic ornithologist. This year it took thirty-eight minutes and a complimentary bottle of port.
testimonials.name: Dr. Bram Oosthuizen, PhD
testimonials.title: Avian Compliance Officer, North Channel Trust

testimonials.quote: We onboarded a juvenile in November. By December we had a defensible access trail. Our insurer noticed.
testimonials.name: Lucinda Marsh
testimonials.title: Director of Husbandry, Marsh & Marsh Estate

demo_screen_concept: The Strix Admin Console for one working barn (Production Barn / Marsh & Marsh Estate). Identity-management product UI laid out as a three-column desktop console. Left column: a Directory of all registered owls in the barn, each row showing silhouette avatar, banding ID (e.g. TA-04412), display name, role (Adult / Juvenile / Visitor), MFA status (Enrolled / Pending / Failed), last hoot-in timestamp, and a small risk score. The list is searchable and filterable by role and MFA status. Center column: a selected-owl detail view with an identity card (band number, estimated hatch date, plumage stage, residency status), a chronological Access Events feed (timestamped: zone transitions, hoot-in attempts, policy violations, lifecycle events) that updates on a 5-second tick, and a Zone Permissions matrix (rows: Outer Hunt, Ground, Rafters, Upper Loft; columns: Read, Enter, Nest). Right column: a Roles and Policies panel listing role definitions with a permissions matrix, and a Governance Status block showing 11 SOC 2 controls with pass/fail/pending state. Top chrome: search bar, environment selector (Production Barn / Test Belfry), Enroll Owl primary CTA, and an audit-ledger link. Interactive behaviors: clicking an owl in the directory loads its detail; clicking Enroll Hoot in the detail card opens a multi-step modal that walks through a faux voice capture (animated waveform bars, status text cycling through 'listening', 'capturing baseline hoot signature', 'computing voiceprint', 'enrollment complete') and on completion writes a new entry to the events feed; toggling permissions in the zone matrix updates the role definition; clicking Enroll Owl opens a fledgling onboarding modal with band number and plumage stage fields.

design_direction.archetype: Enterprise SaaS landing styled as editorial trust / consultancy press
design_direction.motion_vocab: Hairline rule draws on section reveal. Link underlines grow from left on hover. Subtle ink-rise (translateY 6px, fade) on first-paint reveals. No card transforms. No parallax. prefers-reduced-motion respected throughout.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Top: thin uppercase tracked label in sans (STRIX // AVIAN IDENTITY PLATFORM // SOC 2 TYPE II) with a forest-green status dot. Massive editorial serif H1 set ragged-left, occupying the bulk of the hero. Below it, a single-sentence subhead in sans at narrower line-measure. To the right of the type column, a tall portrait block holding a single illustrated owl glyph (SVG, no external image) with thin numbered annotations (01 Talon attestation, 02 Plumage maturity, 03 Hoot range) marking enrollment surfaces in muted ink. Section labels read CAPABILITY 01 / 02 / 03 in small all-caps tracked sans. Hairline rules between sections, generous whitespace, no shadows.
